commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=f1688ea76cca07ba10ed09891410f0400b2c62f3)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a core auto-reply lightweight small-talk admission lane, regression coverage for admission and skill filters, a Codex notification-state test, and a Vitest shard entry for that test.

PR surface: Source +259, Tests +320. Total +579 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as an issue reproduction; this is a feature PR review. Source inspection confirms the prior P1 defects were removed, while the remaining proof gap is latest-head transport behavior.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This needs contributor latest-head proof and maintainer acceptance of a compatibility-sensitive default routing change; automation cannot provide the contributor's real setup proof.

target.title: feat(auto-reply,codex): lightweight reply lane and Codex watchdog
target.body:
## Summary
- add a conservative lightweight reply lane for whole-message obvious small-talk replies
- keep uncertain, mixed-intent, current-info, tool/action, media/link, high-stakes, code/config, unresolved reply-target, and configured skill-filter messages on the full path
- use lightweight bootstrap context without forcing `disableTools`, so runtime/backend tool policy remains with the selected backend
- keep Codex accepted/pre-item turns on the configured attempt timeout so valid startup/prewarm silence is not cut short
- assign the Codex attempt notification-state test to the explicit app-server attempt support shard

## What Problem This Solves
Normal conversational replies were paying the full setup cost even when no special context was needed, while earlier versions of this PR risked over-admitting mixed-intent text and forcing disabled tools before runtime/backend support was known. This version keeps the optimisation limited to whole-message obvious small talk and preserves runtime tool policy.

## Why
- obvious small-talk replies should not pay the full setup cost when no special context is needed
- over-escalation remains harmless because borderline, mixed-intent, current-info, or policy-constrained messages stay on the full agent path
- selected runtimes/backends keep control over tool availability, avoiding always-on native CLI failures
- Codex accepted turns should not be aborted earlier than the configured attempt timeout while startup/prewarm or pre-item work may still be legitimately silent

## Evidence
- Behavior or issue addressed: OpenClaw Telegram replies now have a whole-message positive-match lightweight lane for obvious small talk, while mixed/current-info requests such as “good morning, what’s the weather today?” stay on the full path. The lane no longer forces `disableTools`, so always-on native CLI backends are not given an unsupported no-tool request.
- Existing Mantis proof: native Telegram Desktop before/after proof for an earlier head showed a plain chat reply delivered on both main and the candidate: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/93244#issuecomment-4816610857
- Latest contributor fix on this head: whole-message small-talk matching, mixed/current-info regression coverage, no forced `disableTools`, agent skill filters still block lightweight admission, and accepted Codex turns keep the configured attempt timeout before item-level progress.
- Local proof on latest head:
  ```bash
  pnpm format:check src/auto-reply/reply/lightweight-lane.ts src/auto-reply/reply/lightweight-lane.test.ts
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/lightweight-lane.test.ts
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.test.ts -t "uses merged agent skill policy before admitting the lightweight lane"
  pnpm check:test-types
  ```
- Observed result after fix: formatting passed; lightweight lane tests passed; get-reply skill-policy regression passed; test types passed.
- What is still pending: refreshed hosted GitHub checks and any refreshed Mantis/ClawSweeper verdict for the latest head.

## Tests
- `pnpm format:check src/auto-reply/reply/lightweight-lane.ts src/auto-reply/reply/lightweight-lane.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/lightweight-lane.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.test.ts -t "uses merged agent skill policy before admitting the lightweight lane"`
- `pnpm check:test-types`
